english_query: how two things happening together does not mean one causes the other
ugf_response:
The first thing this person says is that two things can happen at the same time. They can move together or change together in a way that looks like they are tied. The second thing they say is that just because these two things move together, it does not mean the first thing made the second thing happen. They say that we cannot say one thing is the reason for the other just by looking at how they move.

The thing that follows from this is that we must be careful when we see things happening at once. We cannot jump to a quick thought about why things are happening. If we see two things moving together, we might think the first thing is making the second thing happen, but that might not be the truth. We must look for more to find the real reason why things change.

The way this person thinks is good. It is a way of thinking that is very good for the mind. If we always thought that things happening together meant one made the other happen, we would be wrong very often. We would see things that are not real. By being careful and not making a quick guess, we can find the true way that things work. This way of thinking helps us see the world as it really is.

english_query: Yes, for sure.
ugf_response: Well, if you are ready to go on, tell me this: there is something, I think, that you would call what someone knows?

english_query: Either she will take the train or she will take the bus. She will take the train. Therefore, she will not take the bus. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea which says she will take the train or she will take the bus. In normal talk, this often means she picks one or the other, but not both. However, the word 'or' does not always mean you can only pick one. It is possible to take both if the words do not say 'only one'. The second idea says she will take the train. This goes with the first part of the 'or' idea. But knowing she takes the train does not prove she does not take the bus, because the first idea did not say she cannot take both. So, we cannot be sure she leaves the bus behind. The last idea does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: how a person can change who they are by changing what they do
ugf_response:
Person 1: I heard you say that we can change who we are just by changing what we do. How does that work?

Person 2: It is simple. We are made of the things we do every day. If you do new things, you become a new person.

Person 1: But if I am a quiet person, how can doing loud things make me loud? It feels like I am just pretending.

Person 2: At first, it may feel like pretending. But if you keep doing it, your mind starts to think it is normal. You stop seeing it as a act and start seeing it as part of you.

Person 1: So the thing you do comes before the feeling? I always thought I had to feel like it before I could do it.

Person 2: No. The thing you do changes the feeling. If you smile, you start to feel happy. If you run, you start to feel strong. You do not need to wait for the feeling to arrive.

Person 1: That is a big idea. So if I want to be a writer, I just need to write every day?

Person 2: Yes. Do not wait to feel like a writer. Just write. Soon, you will look in the mirror and see a writer looking back.

english_query: why people fear losing more than they enjoy winning the same amount
ugf_response:
Person number one: Why does it feel so bad to lose something, even when getting the same thing would feel good?

Person number two: It is because our minds are built to look for things that can hurt us. Losing feels much more heavy than getting something feels light.

Person number one: But if I find ten pieces of money, I am happy. If I lose ten pieces of money, I am not happy. Why is the bad feeling more than the good feeling?

Person number two: Our brains think that losing is a way to be hurt. When we lose, we feel like we are in trouble. When we get something, we just feel okay. The feeling of being in trouble is very strong.

Person number one: So, is it because we want to stay safe?

Person number two: Yes. In the old days, if a person lost food or a place to sleep, they might die. So, our minds learned to care much more about not losing than about getting more.

Person number one: That makes sense. So, even if the size of the thing is the same, the fear of losing keeps us from trying new things?

Person number two: Yes. We often do not go after good things because we are too afraid of what we might lose. The fear of the thing being gone is bigger than the hope for the win.

english_query: It seems that the part of the great one who speaks out is not different from the great one. For just as the wish of the great one is the cause of things, so is the knowing of the great one. But we do not say that the knowing of the great one speaks out. So we should not say that the wish of the great one speaks out.
ugf_response: What is known is not the cause of a thing being done, unless through wishing. For we do not do what we know, unless we wish to do so. So speaking out is not said of what is known, but of wishing.

english_query: A seasoned but risk‑averse ship captain who values the safety of her crew above profit and prefers to avoid any catastrophic loss.
ugf_response: First, we look at the woman who leads the boat. She has done this job for a long time. She does not like taking big chances. Her main thing she wants is to keep the people who work with her from getting hurt. She cares more about their lives than about getting money. She wants to stay away from any big bad thing that could hurt everyone. Then, we think about what she would do in a hard choice. If she could get more money by going into a storm, she would probably not do it. The chance of losing the boat or the workers is too high. She would rather get less money and stay safe. Someone might say that a good leader should try to get the most money for the owners. But this leader puts her workers first. So, the idea that she would take a big chance for money does not fit her. She would choose the path that has the least chance of a terrible end. This means she will avoid trouble even if it takes away some of her money. So, her moves will always point toward keeping people well over getting rich. The leader will choose the safest path to keep her workers safe, even if it means getting less money.

english_query: the problem of spending more just because you already spent a lot
ugf_response:
The person is talking about a way of thinking that leads to a bad choice. The first idea is that a person has already given away a lot of money or time to something. The second idea is that they feel they must keep giving more because they have already given so much. They think that if they stop now, the things they already gave will have been for nothing.

The thing they think follows from this is that they should keep going. They believe that adding more money or more time will make the first part of their work or spending good again. They think that more spending will turn a bad start into a good thing.

We must look at if this way of thinking is good. This way of thinking is not good. It is a way that is not right in how a person thinks. The things you did in the past are gone. You cannot get them back. When you decide what to do next, you should only look at what will happen from now on. You should not look at what you did before. If you spend more just because you spent a lot before, you are only making the thing you do not have even bigger. You are not making the first part of the spending have any use. This is a bad way to make a choice.

english_query: Also, it is written: 'We see now through a glass in a dark way; but then face to face.' This shows there are two ways to know God. One is seeing Him in what He really is, which is called seeing face to face. The other is seeing Him in the mirror of made things. As was shown before, a spirit thing cannot have the first way by his own born power. And the second way, seeing through a mirror, does not belong to spirit things, because they do not get their knowing of God from things we can see, as an old teacher able to think well says. So spirit things cannot know God by their born powers.
ugf_response: The knowing a spirit thing has of God is in the middle between these two kinds of knowing; still, it is closer to one of them, as was said above.

english_query: why different situations need different kinds of good character
ugf_response:
To be a good person is not a thing that stays the same all the time. A good person must change how they act on how things are around them. Think about a person who looks after a small child. For that person, being good means being very soft and very kind. They must be able to wait for a long time without getting mad and give much care. If that same person goes to a fight to keep their home safe, being soft and kind would not be good. In a fight, being good might mean being strong and being hard. If they are too soft during a fight, they might let bad things happen to those they love.

We can see this by looking at different parts of life. First, think about a person who works with many other people to build something big. For this person, being good means giving everyone the same thing and being able to work well with others. They must listen and follow the way things are done. Second, think about a person who must make a choice that will change everything for a whole group of people. For this person, being good means being very careful and thinking about what will happen a long time from now. They cannot just think about what feels good right now.

If a person only has one way of being good, they will fail when the world changes. If someone is always very loud and strong, they will hurt people when they should be quiet and kind. If someone is always very quiet and follows what others say, they will not be able to stand up when something bad is happening. Being a good person means knowing which part of your heart to use at the right time. You must look at the world, see what is needed, and then use the right way of being. To be very good, you must be able to change your ways to fit the world around you.

english_query: Either the runner finished first or the runner finished second. The runner did not finish first. Therefore, the runner finished second. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea which says one of two things must be true: the runner finished first or the runner finished second. Then, the second idea tells us that the runner did not finish first. Since the first idea says it has to be one or the other, and we know it is not the first one, the only thing left is that the runner finished second. So, the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: the way to help yourself think about hard questions step by step
ugf_response:
When you want to know something that is hard to know, you must not try to find the answer all at once. If you try to do too much, your head will feel full and you will not find the truth. Instead, you must take the big thing and make it into many small things.

First, you must look at the big thing and find the smallest parts that make it up. If you want to know why something happens, do not look at the whole world. Look at one small piece of it. Once you see that piece, ask yourself what it is doing.

Second, you must look at how these small parts work with each other. You can see how the first small piece touches the second small piece. You can see if the first piece makes the second piece move or change. You must do this for every piece until you see the whole picture made of these small parts.

Third, you must check if what you think is true. You should not just believe what you want to believe. You must look for things that show you are wrong. If you find something that does not fit, you must change how you think about the parts. You must go back and look at the small parts again.

The thing after the third thing is this. You must be careful not to let your own feelings change what you see. Sometimes we want something to be true so much that we see things that are not there. To stop this, you must act as if you are looking at a thing from far away. You must look at the parts as they are, not as you wish they were.

If you do this, you will not be lost in the big thing. You will move through the small things one by one. By taking care of each small thing, you will slowly find the answer to the big thing.

english_query: Either the door is open or the door is locked. The door is not open. Therefore, the door is locked. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says one of two things must be true. The first thing is that the door is open. The second thing is that the door is locked. The word or here means one of these has to be the case. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us that the door is not open. This means the first part of the first idea is not true. Now we check what is left. If the door is not open, and it must be either open or locked, then it has to be locked. We can try a small story to see if this holds. Imagine a room. The door is either open or locked. You look and see the door is not open. Can you say the door is locked? Yes, because there are no other choices given. So the last idea must be true if the first two are true. The last part follows for sure. follows

english_query: Do the person who knows a lot and the person who does not know, and the person who is not afraid and the person who is afraid, have the same size of good feeling and bad feeling? Or would you say that the person who is afraid has more?
ugf_response: I should say that he does.

english_query: If the door is unlocked, then the door can be opened from outside. The door is unlocked. Therefore, the door can be opened from outside. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we see the first idea. It says if the door is not locked, then we can open it from the outside. Next, we see the second idea. It says the door is not locked. This is the same as the first part of the if-then thought. So, the second part must be true too. This means we can open the door from the outside. follows

english_query: It looks like in god nothing is bad or not perfect. But it looks not perfect for a person who lead a land to lead by using other people; so a person who lead a land, because he cannot do all things himself and cannot be in all places at the same time, must lead by help from other people. So god lead all things right away.
ugf_response: That a person who lead a land has help to do the things he says people should do is a sign not only that he is not perfect but also that he is important; because by the order of help the power of the person who lead a land is shown more.

english_query: If she takes the medicine, then her headache goes away. Her headache does not go away. Therefore, she does not take the medicine. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea: if she takes the thing to help her head, then her bad head pain stops. This tells us what happens when she does take that thing. Next, we look at the second idea: her bad head pain does not stop. We know that if she had taken that thing, the pain would be gone. Since the pain is not gone, she could not have taken that thing. If she had, the pain would be gone, but it is not. So, the last idea says she does not take that thing. This is the same as what we found. The last idea follows for sure from the first ideas. follows

english_query: why counting up good and bad results is harder than it sounds
ugf_response:
We see a group of people look at a set of things that have some good parts and some bad parts. They try to make a number of the good parts and a number of the bad parts. At first it may seem like an easy step: just put a sign for each good thing and each bad thing and then add them up. But when they sit down to do it, they find that the work is not as clear as it first looks.

One reason is that the good and bad parts are not always clear. A thing that looks good at one time may turn bad later, and a thing that looks bad at one time may turn good later. People may also see the same thing in different ways. One person may say a thing is good because it helps them, while another may say it is bad because it hurts someone else. Because of this, the signs that each person puts down can change, and the final numbers can end up far from what any one person first thought.

Another reason is that the way we add the signs can change the story we tell. If we simply count each good sign as one and each bad sign as one, we lose the sense that some good things may be bigger or more important than others. Some people will try to give a bigger count to the big good things, while others will keep every count the same. This makes the final numbers look different as the way changes.

People will say many things about why this is not easy. Some will say that the hardest part is that we cannot see all the good and bad parts at once, so we have to guess about the ones we do not see. Others will say that the hardest part is that each person brings a different view, so the signs they put down are not the same. Still others will say that the hardest part is that the way we add the signs can hide the real size of the good and bad parts.

In the end, the ask is not just to make a number of good and a number of bad, but to think about how we see each part, how we decide what counts as good or bad, and how we add them up. The many replies show that the easy act of making a count can turn into a deep look at how we see the world and how we try to put that view into a simple number.

english_query: Either the package arrives today or the package arrives tomorrow. The package arrives today. Therefore, the package does not arrive tomorrow. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says one of two things must be true. The first thing is that the box comes today. The second thing is that the box comes tomorrow. This means at least one of these things is true. It does not say that only one can be true. It just says one or the other or both. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us for sure that the box comes today. This fits with the first part of the first idea. Now we ask if the box can also come tomorrow. The first idea allows for the box to come today. It does not stop the box from coming tomorrow too. For a small story, imagine the box is big and part of it comes today and part of it comes tomorrow. Or maybe the first idea just means it is ready today, but it could still be there tomorrow. Since the first idea does not say the two things cannot both happen, we cannot be sure that the second thing is not true. We know the first thing is true, but that does not force the second thing to be not true. So, the last idea does not follow for sure from the first ideas. does not follow

english_query: why people sometimes pick the worse thing even when they know better
ugf_response:
When a person sees two ways to act, one that will bring more good later and one that looks easier now, they sometimes still go for the easier way even though they know the other way is better. This can happen in small things like choosing a small piece of food, or in big things like choosing a job or a person they live with. The person may feel a pull toward the easy thing and then move in that direction.  

The hard question is why a mind that knows the better way can still pick the worse way. What makes the pull of the now so strong that it can beat the thought of later good? This question has no simple yes or no, and many people have tried to talk about it.  

One answer that people give is that the feeling of now feels safe and not making you feel afraid. The brain can think that a change may bring pain or the thing that is taken away, so it stays with what it knows. Another answer is that the person may want a quick good feeling now and not wait for a bigger good later. The quick good feeling can be a sweet feeling in the mouth, a laugh, a look, or a feeling of being okay for a short time. A third answer is that past hurt can make a person trust the easy thing more than the new thing, because the new thing may have caused hurt before. Another answer is that the person may not see the later good clearly; the picture of the later good can be not clear, and the brain may pick the thing it can see right now.  

One more view is that the draw of feeling can be strong enough that the mind does not use the clear thought about later good. The mind may be busy with many other things, and the draw of now can win. This is why we see people sometimes pick the worse thing even when they know better.

english_query: It looks like not all things are under the care of god. For nothing that is seen before can happen by chance. If god saw all things before, nothing would happen by chance. And so chance would go away; which goes against what most people think.
ugf_response: There is a big change between a cause for one thing and a cause for all. A thing can get away from the plan of a cause for one thing; but not from the plan of a cause for all. For nothing gets away from the plan of a cause for one thing, except when another cause for one thing stops it; like water stopping wood from burning. Since all causes for one thing are under the cause for all, it could not be that any end happens outside the reach of that cause for all. So when an end gets away from the plan of a cause for one thing, we say it is by chance for that cause; but if we look at the cause for all, outside whose reach no end can happen, we say it was seen before. So, for one case, two workers meeting, although to them it looks like a chance thing, was seen before by their leader, who sent them to meet at the same place, in such a way that one did not know about the other.

english_query: Also, in god there is no real way to tell them as not the same but that of coming from. But one way of being toward does not seem to come from another. So the ways of being toward are not really told as not the same from each other.
ugf_response: Even though ways of being toward, for true, do not come or go from each other, still they are seen as against each other by the going of one from another.

english_query: Explain why the lord might reject a low offer even though it gives him more gold than nothing, and how the advisors’ just‑world belief changes the optimal offer you should make.
ugf_response: First, we look at the man with power. He might say no to a small pile of gold. Even if some gold is better than none, he might feel the offer is not enough for his standing. If he takes it, he might look bad to others. So, he might throw the gold away to show he will not be pushed around. He cares more about his name than the metal. Next, we think about the men who talk to him. They believe the world is right and good. They think good people get good things. If they think the man with power is good, they think he should get a big offer. If you give a small offer, they think you are wrong. They will tell him to say no because they think it is right to be given good things. This changes what you should do. If you know they think this way, you should not give a small offer. You should give a bigger one that looks right to them. If you give too little, they will make him say no, and you get nothing. So, you must give enough to make them happy. The man with power may say no to a small offer to save face, even if it means losing gold. Because his helpers believe the world is right, they will push him to say no to offers they see as wrong, so you should make a larger offer to please them.

english_query: Commitment devices improve outcomes in strategic settings by making threats or promises credible. By voluntarily limiting one’s own future actions, a player can change the opponent’s expectations, causing the opponent to choose a more favorable response. Types include contracts, reputation, precommitment (burning bridges), delegation, and moral rules. The device works by altering payoffs so that the committed action becomes optimal at the decision node, allowing backward induction to select a better equilibrium for the commit‑ter.
ugf_response: First, we see that in games, people often say they will do something, but then they change their mind when it is time to act. This makes their word not strong. If a person does something to stop themselves from changing their mind, like breaking a bridge so they cannot go back, the other person sees this. The other person knows the first person cannot do anything else. So, the other person believes the first person. Because of this belief, the other person acts in a way that is good for the first person. Some might say it is bad to stop yourself from doing what you want. But if you can do anything, the other person does not trust you, and you get a bad ending. By stopping yourself, you make your promise real, and you get a better ending. Stopping yourself from changing your mind makes others believe you, which leads to a better ending for you in a game.

english_query: why what you already believe changes how you see new things
ugf_response:
Imagine you are wearing a pair of glasses with colored glass. If the glass is red, everything you look at will seem to have a red color on it. This is how your mind works when you see something for the first time.

The things you already think are true act like these colored glasses. When you see something new, your mind does not look at it in a way that is completely free. Instead, your mind tries to fit the new thing into the things you already know.

Think about it this way. Suppose you believe that all dogs are bad and will bite you. If you see a new dog walking toward you, you will look for signs that it is mean. You might see it move its mouth and think it is getting ready to bite. You might see it run and think it is coming to hurt you. Because you already have a thought in your head that dogs are bad, your eyes and your mind work together to find things that make that thought feel right.

On the other hand, if you believe that all dogs are kind and want to play, you will see that same dog in a different way. You might see it move its mouth and think it is happy. You might see it run and think it wants to play with you. The dog has not changed at all. The dog is the very same dog in both stories. What changed was the way your mind looked at the dog.

Your mind likes to feel that it is right. It does not like to feel that it is wrong. When something new comes to you, your mind quickly asks, Does this fit with what I already know? If it fits, your mind says, Yes, this is true. If it does not fit, your mind might try to change how it sees the new thing so that it does fit. This happens without you even knowing it.

This means that two people can look at the very same thing and see two different things. One person sees one thing because of what they believe. The other person sees a different thing because of what they believe. We do not see the world as it is. We see the world as we are.

english_query: If the team practiced all week, then the team plays well on Saturday. If the team plays well on Saturday, then the car can move. Therefore, if the team practiced all week, then the car can move. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the team did work all week, then they do good on the day after the work day. This sets up a path. Second, we look at the next idea. It says if the team does good on that day, then the car can go. This joins the doing good to the car going. Now we put these two ideas together. If the team did work, they do good. If they do good, the car goes. So, if the team did work, the car goes. The last idea says exactly this: if the team did work all week, then the car can go. We can make a small story to check this. Suppose the team did work. Then they must do good. Since they do good, the car must go. There is no way for the team to work and for the car not to go, given these things. So the last idea follows for sure from the first ideas. follows

english_query: In the guild apprentice workshop, two senior craftsmen present you with a choice about how to allocate the guild's resources for the upcoming festival.
ugf_response: First, we look at the two choices. One is to spend gold on a big party. The other is to keep the gold to fix the things we use to make stuff. If we pick the party, we are happy now. But later, we have no way to make new things. If we pick the fixing, we miss the fun now. But we can make things next year. Some might say fun is good. But if we cannot make things, we cannot get more gold. So, the strong answer is to fix the making things. This helps us more in the long run. We should choose to fix the things we use to make stuff. This keeps our work going for the future.

english_query: Also, the old teacher of many says that the picture in the mind is to the thinking part what color is to seeing. But seeing does not happen by taking the idea of color out of the color, but by the color making its mark on the eye. So thinking does not happen by taking something out of the picture in the mind, but by the picture making its mark on the thinking part.
ugf_response: Colors, being in real body things, are like the power of seeing: so they can make their mark on the eye. But pictures in the mind, since they are marks of single things and live in body parts, are not like the human thinking part, so they cannot make their mark on the waiting thinking part by themselves. This is done by the power of the working thinking part which turns to the picture and makes a certain mark in the waiting thinking part that shows, only in its main way, the thing seen in the picture. This is how we say the idea is taken out of the picture; not that the same mark that was in the picture is then in the waiting thinking part, like a thing moved from one place to another.

english_query: Really old teacher, I say that I did not mean that. I only asked the question because I do it often; but if you can show me that what you say is true, I want you to.
ugf_response: It will not be easy, but I will try to make you happy as much as I can. Let us say that you call one of your many helpers, so I can show this to him.

english_query: No, not in this way of saying it, old teacher.
ugf_response: Then I spoke the truth, hot one, that not you, nor I, nor any person, would want to do wrong more than to have wrong done to them; for doing wrong is the bigger bad thing of the two.

english_query: Also, the dark time comes between the end of the day and the start of the next; while the middle of the day comes between the start and the end. So if the good spirits have a start-of-day knowing and an end-of-day knowing, it looks like they should also have a middle-of-day knowing and a dark-time knowing.
ugf_response: The start-of-day and end-of-day knowing are part of the light time, which is for the good spirits who are far from the dark time, which is for the bad spirits. The good spirits, while they know about things made, do not stick to them, for that would be to turn to the dark time and the night; but they point this back to saying good things about God, in whom, as in their start, they know all things. So after the end-of-day there is no dark time, but a start-of-day; so the start-of-day is the end of the last light time, and the start of the next, as far as the spirits point to saying good things about God their knowing of the last work. The middle-of-day is part of the light time, as the middle between the two ends. Or else the middle can be about their knowing of God himself, who has no start and no end.

english_query: Brother Thomas sighs, "It seems the numbers still puzzle you. Remember, probabilities must add up to exactly one. Try adding the four values again and see what you get."
ugf_response: First, we look at what the brother says. He tells us that the chances of all things happening must add up to one. This is how we count chance. If the numbers do not add to one, then something is wrong. The person is asked to add the four things again. This means the first time they added them, they might have made a wrong move. We must trust this way of counting because it tells us how chance works. If the adding up is less than one, we are missing some things. If the adding up is more than one, we are counting things twice or using wrong numbers. So, the thing to do is to find the right adding up that is one. The hard part is fixed by fixing the counting to fit the idea. The four things must be added in a right way so that their adding up is exactly one, because that is how chance works.

english_query: why a true belief for bad reasons is not the same as knowing
ugf_response:
The person is talking about a way of thinking. The first idea is that a person can have a thought that is true. This person thinks something is true, and it is actually true in the real world. The second idea is that the person has this true thought for bad reasons. This means the way they got to that thought was not good or was not right. english_query: the problem with thinking only two choices exist when there are more
ugf_response: When a person looks at a situation and says that there are only two ways to go, that can cause a lot of trouble. First, the mind may stop looking for any other ways. The mind can get stuck in a small space and not see other things that could be done. When the mind only sees two ways, it may think that one of them must be right and the other must be wrong. That can make the person feel angry or sad if the way they do not like is the one they have to take. It can also make them fight with other people who think the other way is the right one. Second, when there are more ways than two, missing the extra ways can lead to a worse end. If a person only looks at the two ways and picks the one that seems best, they may miss a third way that would have been even better. The person may end up with a bad end for them or for the ones around them, simply because they did not see the third way. Third, thinking that only two ways are there can keep a group from working together. If each person in a group thinks the other side has only the other way, they may not try to find a middle way that could help both sides. The group may stay in a fight instead of finding a new way that works for many. To avoid this trouble, it helps to keep the mind open. When you hear a problem, try to ask yourself if there is a thing you could do that is not the first way and not the second way. Look for any other ways, even if they seem small or new. Talk with other people and ask them what they think could be done. Often, when you hear the idea of another person, you will see a new way that you did not think of before. By doing this, you give yourself more chances to find a good end. You also make it easier to work with other people, because you are not stuck on the idea that there are only two ways. The mind stays able to move, the group stays calm, and the end can be better for everyone.

english_query: how the kind of person you are matters more than following rules
ugf_response:
First, we see that a person looks at the ways many other people say you should act and also thinks about who they are inside, about the sort of person they want to be, and about what feels right to them. The situation is that many people tell us there are certain set ways that we should follow, like a group of things that are said to be good to do, and at the same time we hear that the kind of person we are, the sort of good or kind feelings we hold, might be even more important than just doing the same set ways.

The hard question that comes up is this: when a choice must be made, should a person try first to be the sort of good, caring person they feel they are, even if that means not doing exactly what the set ways say, or should they try first to do exactly what the set ways tell them, even if that sometimes feels like it does not fit the feeling of being a good person inside? The hard part is that both sides seem to have good reasons. On one hand, the idea of being a good person inside can show us to act in ways that help others and keep us telling what is true, but on the other hand, the set ways are there to keep many people safe and to make life easier for a large group, and breaking them can cause a bad state or hurt.

One group of people says that the sort of person you are matters more. They say that if you are kind, telling what is true, and caring in your heart, you will usually find a way to act that is right for the situation, even if it means not following a set way that feels wrong. They point to times when the set ways have not been right or have caused pain, and they say that a good inside the person can see those problems and act better.

Another group says that the set ways matter more. They say that when many people follow the same ways, it makes life easy to guess and safe, and that a single person feeling of being good can be different for each person, which can lead to big trouble if everyone just follows their own feeling. They note that the set ways often come from a long time of trying to keep groups together.

A third group tries to put together the two ideas. They say that a person should try to be good inside, but also try to follow the set ways unless those ways clearly cause hurt or go against what feels right. They say that this middle lets us keep the being safe of the group while also being true to the sort of person we want to be.

These different answers come from what each person sees in their own life. Those who have felt hurt by not right set ways may trust their inside good feeling more. Those who have lived in places where breaking the set ways caused trouble may trust the set ways more. And those who have seen both good and bad in each side may try to hold both ideas together, hoping to keep order while still being true to the sort of person they want to be.

Person 2: We also look at how many people are part of that change.

Person 2: Then we try to add the small changes together and see if they add up to more than the one big change.  
Person 1: Is there an easy way to do that?  
Person 2: We can make a group of notes, write each need, give each a number for its size, then add the numbers.

english_query: Ask the player to compute the expected payoffs for each strategy given a specific p (e.g., p = 0.4) and compare them. Then guide them to set the two expressions equal to solve for p.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first part of the job. We need to find the usual win for each way to play. We do this by taking the chance of winning, which is the first thing, and making it bigger by the money won, then adding the chance of losing times the money lost. If the first thing is 0.4, we put that number in and get a clear group of money for each choice. Then we see which choice gives more money. Next, we look at the second part. We want to find the special size of the first thing where both choices give the same group of money. To do this, we write the number talk for the first choice and say it is the same as the number talk for the second choice. Then we move the numbers around to find the first thing. This tells us the point where it does not matter which choice you make. Someone might say this is too hard, but it is just simple number talk with letters. It helps us see when one choice is better than the other. To do this, find the usual win for each choice using the first thing is 0.4 to see which is better. Then, set the two usual win number talks the same as each other and find the first thing to see where they are the same.

english_query: why some things you must never do to another person no matter what
ugf_response:
The person says that there are some ways to act toward other people that you must never do. This is the first idea. They say that even if doing a bad thing would bring a great good to many people, you still must not do that bad thing to one person. This is the second idea. They think that if we say it is okay to hurt one person to help many, then we lose the ground we stand on for what is good. This is the third idea. They think that if we do not have these things that we must never do, then no one is safe at all. This is the idea after the third idea.

Now we look at what they think follows from these ideas. They think that we must have a set of things that are always wrong. They think that we cannot just look at how much good comes from an act to see if the act is good. Instead, they think we must look at the act itself. If the act is a thing that should never be done, then it is wrong even if it makes many people happy.

Now we look at whether the thinking is good. To see if the thinking is good, we must see if the first and second ideas lead to the third and the idea after the third in a way that makes sense. The first idea is a starting point. The second idea tells us how to use that starting point. If we say some things are always wrong, then it follows that we cannot use those things to get a good end. This part of the thinking is strong because the steps go one after the other in a way that fits. However, one might ask if the first idea is real and true. If we cannot agree that some things are always wrong, then the whole way of thinking falls down. The thinking is good if you agree with the starting point, but it is hard to show that the starting point is the only way to see the world.

english_query: Either the runner finished first or the runner finished second. The runner did not finish first. Therefore, the runner finished second. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says one of two things must be true. Either the runner came in first place, or the runner came in second place. There are no other ways this can be. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us that the runner did not come in first place. This means the first choice is not true. Since one of the two choices had to be true, and the first one is out, the second choice must be the one that is true. So, the runner must have come in second place. We can try to see if this makes sense by thinking of a small story. Imagine the first two ideas are right. Can the last idea be wrong? If the runner did not come first, and the only other way was second, then saying the runner did not come second would mean the runner came in some other place, like third. But the first idea said only first or second were possible. So that story cannot happen. The last idea has to be right if the first two are right. So this means the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: It seems that god does not stay the same. Because whatever changes itself is in some way able to change. But, as the great old church teacher says, the maker moves itself not by time and not by place. So god is in some way able to change.
ugf_response: The great old church teacher speaks here like the old man who said the first mover moves itself; calling every doing a moving, even as the acts of thinking, and wanting, and caring, are called moving. So because god thinks and cares for itself, in that way they said that god moves itself, not, however, as moving and changing belong to a thing that can be changed, as we now speak of changing and moving.

english_query: It seems that in made things there is not always a mark of the three-in-one. Because we can find the thing by its marks. But we cannot find the three-in-one from made things, as was said before. So there is no mark of the three-in-one in made things.
ugf_response: The mark points to how we give names to the parts: in this way we can know the three-in-one of the god parts from made things, as we have said.

english_query: If he turns on the light, then the room is bright. He does not turn on the light. Therefore, the room is not bright. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the man turns on the light then the room is bright. That means that turning on the light is a way to make the room bright. Second, we look at the second idea. It says the man does not turn on the light. From these two ideas we only know that the way of turning on the light is not used. But the room could still be bright because there might be another way, like a window or the sun. So we can not be sure that the room is not bright. The last idea does not have to be true. does not follow

english_query: Either she will take the train or she will take the bus. She will take the train. Therefore, she will not take the bus. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea which says she will take one of two things: the train or the bus. This makes a plan where only one happens. The second idea tells us for sure that she will take the train. In this kind of thinking, if you pick one side of an either-or choice, the other side is left out. Since she is taking the train, she cannot be taking the bus at the same time in this simple plan. So the last idea that she will not take the bus follows for sure from the first two ideas. follows

english_query: Also, because time is the outside way we count things that are made, place is the same. So place, just like time, must be counted as one of the first things that were made.
ugf_response: Place is thought to be in the high sky, which is the edge of all space. Because place talks about things that always stay, it was made all at once. But time does not always stay, so it was made at its start: we can only feel a part of time that is the now.

english_query: If the fire has fuel, then the fire keeps burning. The fire goes out. Therefore, the fire has no fuel. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the fire has the food it needs to burn, then the fire keeps burning. The second idea tells us that the fire stops. This means the fire is not keeping burning. Now we check if the last idea must be true. The last idea says the fire has no food for burning. We know that if there was food for burning, the fire would burn. Since the fire is not burning, it seems possible there is no food for burning. But wait, could there be another reason the fire stopped? Maybe the air was taken away, or maybe water was thrown on it. The first idea only tells us what happens when the food for burning is there. It does not say that the food for burning is the only thing that matters. So, it is possible that the fire had the food for burning but still stopped because of something else. If that were true, the fire would have the food for burning, yet it would stop. This would mean the first idea is still true, but the last idea would be wrong. Because we can imagine a story where the fire has the food for burning but stops for a different reason, the last idea does not have to be true. It does not follow for sure. does not follow
